message.content:
I need to evaluate which item has lower maintenance cost (cheaper to maintain as code and models evolve).

**Item A (when_not_to_use_cardinal):** A section explaining when NOT to use scalar metrics, deterministic sorting, or whole-list ranking. This is conceptual/advisory content. It describes conditions and trade-offs. As code and models evolve, this content is relatively stable - the fundamental reasons to avoid certain approaches don't change much. It's prose/documentation that doesn't depend on specific API signatures or model names.

**Item B (public_api_recipe):** A minimal library example that constructs a `MultiRerankRequest`, installs a model policy, runs one attribute, and emits a report. This is code that depends on:
- Specific class names (`MultiRerankRequest`)
- API signatures (constructor parameters, method names)
- Model policy interfaces
- Report emission APIs

As code and models evolve, this recipe will break or become outdated whenever:
- The API changes (class renames, parameter changes)
- Model policies change
- Report formats change
- The library version updates

Item A is conceptual guidance that remains valid across API versions. Item B is tightly coupled to specific implementation details that will change as the codebase evolves.

Item A has significantly higher maintenance cheapness (lower maintenance cost). The ratio reflects that B requires updates with every API change while A is largely evergreen.

{"higher_id":"A","ratio":4.0,"confidence":0.78}
